query_normalized: investigating the impact of network centrality on resting-state functional connectivity in autism spectrum disorder neuroscience
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (e) as it investigates functional brain network topology and connectivity patterns specifically within the Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) population, which is the exact empirical domain and mechanism (resting-state/functional connectivity) the user is studying, even though it focuses on state-dependent changes rather than centrality metrics specifically.
